content.method: Administrative record review of closure/suspension orders 2013-2026 coded by initiating actor and outcome (permanent/reversed), supplemented with case-file analysis of documented reversals and their triggering conditions (petition size, media coverage, political timing).
content.question: What proportion of recent Hungarian village school closures were mandated by central tankerület policy versus initiated at municipal/principal discretion citing unsustainability, and what proportion of closures/suspensions have been reversed, and under what conditions?
content.why_it_matters: This determines whether procedural safeguards (T1) or evidence registries (T6) target the actual decision locus, and whether informal protest-driven reversal (as in Erdőkürt, Budapest 2025) is a scalable mechanism (T4) or an idiosyncratic exception.
